name: Voyager 2
first: Only spacecraft to visit Uranus and Neptune; Grand Tour of four giant planets
description: The twin that kept going when budgets tightened, Voyager 2 carried the Grand Tour from Jupiter and Saturn out to Uranus and Neptune — still the only close-up images we have of the ice giants. Its Neptune encounter in 1989 revealed the Great Dark Spot and Triton's geysers. Today both Voyagers sail in interstellar space with a golden record as humanity's calling card. Orrery lists Neptune as the catalogue destination; the real path relied on multiple gravity assists.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Titan IIIE lifts Voyager 2 from Cape Canaveral on a Grand-Tour trajectory.

events.label: NEPTUNE CLOSEST APPROACH
events.note: Flyby of Neptune and large moon Triton; last planetary encounter of the Grand Tour.
